documents: Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., 2017): introduced the Transformer architecture based on self-attention.
BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for language understanding (Devlin et al., 2019).
Language Models are Few-Shot Learners (Brown et al., 2020): the GPT-3 paper demonstrating in-context learning.
A call for reproducibility in machine learning (Pineau et al., 2021): checklists and reporting standards.
Measurement invariance and benchmarking pitfalls in NLP evaluation.
Cross-lingual representational alignment: methods and limitations for multilingual models.
Federated optimisation in heterogeneous networks (Li et al., 2020): the FedProx algorithm for non-IID data.
RoBERTa: A Robustly Optimized BERT Pretraining Approach (Liu et al., 2019): longer training, more data.
Vision Transformer (Dosovitskiy et al., 2021): applying the Transformer to image patches for classification.
Reformer: The Efficient Transformer (Kitaev et al., 2020): locality-sensitive hashing to reduce attention cost.
A survey of data augmentation techniques for low-resource natural language processing.
Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models (Kaplan et al., 2020): loss scales as a power law with model size.
Deduplicating training data makes language models better (Lee et al., 2022).
The reproducibility crisis in psychology: implications for empirical science broadly.
Communication-efficient learning of deep networks from decentralized data (McMahan et al., 2017): original FedAvg.
Massively multilingual sentence embeddings for zero-shot cross-lingual transfer (LASER).
GLUE: A multi-task benchmark and analysis platform for natural language understanding.
Electra: pre-training text encoders as discriminators rather than generators (Clark et al., 2020).
Random seed variability in deep reinforcement learning results (Henderson et al., 2018).
Differentially private federated learning: privacy budgets across hospital sites.

queries.query: who introduced the original transformer architecture with self-attention?
queries.note: distractors: ViT, Reformer, RoBERTa, Electra all mention transformers/attention

queries.query: efficient attention that reduces the quadratic cost of transformers
queries.note: distractor: Vaswani transformer, ViT both about attention but not efficiency

queries.query: applying transformers to images instead of text
in-context few-shot learning with very large language models
queries.note: distractor: scaling laws paper also about large LMs

queries.query: how does model performance scale with parameter count?
queries.note: distractor: GPT-3 few-shot paper also about large models

queries.query: reproducibility standards and checklists for ML research
queries.note: distractors: psychology reproducibility crisis, RL seed variability

queries.query: seed sensitivity and variance in reinforcement learning results
queries.note: distractor: Pineau ML reproducibility, psychology crisis

queries.query: the original federated averaging algorithm on decentralized data
handling non-IID data across clients in federated learning
zero-shot transfer across languages using multilingual embeddings
pitfalls and invariance problems in NLP benchmark evaluation
a robustly optimized BERT with longer training
